2.text: And it came to pass, when the LORD<S>3068</S> would take up<S>5927</S> Elijah<S>452</S> into heaven<S>8064</S> by a whirlwind<S>5591</S>, that Elijah<S>452</S> went<S>3212</S> with Elisha<S>477</S> from Gilgal<S>1537</S>.

And they rose up early<S>7925</S> in the morning<S>1242</S>, and the sun<S>8121</S> shone<S>2224</S> upon the water<S>4325</S>, and the Moabites<S>4124</S> saw<S>7200</S> the water<S>4325</S> on the other side<S>5048</S> as red<S>122</S> as blood<S>1818</S>:

4.text: Now there cried<S>6817</S> a certain<S>259</S> woman<S>802</S> of the wives<S>802</S> of the sons<S>1121</S> of the prophets<S>5030</S> unto Elisha<S>477</S>, saying<S>559</S>, Thy servant<S>5650</S> my husband<S>376</S> is dead<S>4191</S>; and thou knowest<S>3045</S> that thy servant<S>5650</S> did fear<S>3373</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>: and the creditor<S>5383</S> is come<S>935</S> to take<S>3947</S> unto him my two<S>8147</S> sons<S>3206</S> to be bondmen<S>5650</S>.

And it fell on a day<S>3117</S>, that Elisha<S>477</S> passed<S>5674</S> to Shunem<S>7766</S>, where was a great<S>1419</S> woman<S>802</S>; and she constrained<S>2388</S> him to eat<S>398</S> bread<S>3899</S>. Let us make<S>6213</S> a little<S>6996</S> chamber<S>5944</S>, I pray thee, on the wall<S>7023</S>; and let us set<S>7760</S> for him there a bed<S>4296</S>, and a table<S>7979</S>, and a stool<S>3678</S>, and a candlestick<S>4501</S>: and it shall be, when he cometh<S>935</S> to us, that he shall turn<S>5493</S> in thither.

And he said<S>559</S> unto him, Say<S>559</S> now unto her, Behold, thou hast been careful<S>2729</S> for us with all this care<S>2731</S>; what is to be done<S>6213</S> for thee? wouldest<S>3426</S> thou be spoken<S>1696</S> for to the king<S>4428</S>, or to the captain<S>8269</S> of the host<S>6635</S>? And he said<S>559</S>, Wherefore wilt thou go<S>1980</S> to him to day<S>3117</S>? it is neither new moon<S>2320</S>, nor sabbath<S>7676</S>. And she said<S>559</S>, It shall be well<S>7965</S>. <sup>well: Heb. peace</sup>

Then she saddled<S>2280</S> an ass<S>860</S>, and said<S>559</S> to her servant<S>5288</S>, Drive<S>5090</S>, and go forward<S>3212</S>; slack<S>6113</S> not thy riding<S>7392</S> for me, except I bid<S>559</S> thee. <sup>slack: Heb. restrain not for me to ride</sup>

Run<S>7323</S> now, I pray thee, to meet<S>7125</S> her, and say<S>559</S> unto her, Is it well<S>7965</S> with thee? is it well<S>7965</S> with thy husband<S>376</S>? is it well<S>7965</S> with the child<S>3206</S>? And she answered<S>559</S>, It is well<S>7965</S>.

And when she came<S>935</S> to the man<S>376</S> of God<S>430</S> to the hill<S>2022</S>, she caught<S>2388</S> him by the feet<S>7272</S>: but Gehazi<S>1522</S> came near<S>5066</S> to thrust her away<S>1920</S>. And the man<S>376</S> of God<S>430</S> said<S>559</S>, Let her alone<S>7503</S>; for her soul<S>5315</S> is vexed<S>4843</S> within her: and the LORD<S>3068</S> hath hid<S>5956</S> it from me, and hath not told<S>5046</S> me. <sup>him: Heb. by his feet</sup> <sup>vexed: Heb. bitter</sup>
Then she said<S>559</S>, Did I desire<S>7592</S> a son<S>1121</S> of my lord<S>113</S>? did I not say<S>559</S>, Do not deceive<S>7952</S> me?

Then he said<S>559</S> to Gehazi<S>1522</S>, Gird up<S>2296</S> thy loins<S>4975</S>, and take<S>3947</S> my staff<S>4938</S> in thine hand<S>3027</S>, and go thy way<S>3212</S>: if thou meet<S>4672</S> any man<S>376</S>, salute<S>1288</S> him not; and if any<S>376</S> salute<S>1288</S> thee, answer him not again<S>6030</S>: and lay<S>7760</S> my staff<S>4938</S> upon the face<S>6440</S> of the child<S>5288</S>.

And he called<S>7121</S> Gehazi<S>1522</S>, and said<S>559</S>, Call<S>7121</S> this Shunammite<S>7767</S>. So he called<S>7121</S> her. And when she was come in<S>935</S> unto him, he said<S>559</S>, Take up<S>5375</S> thy son<S>1121</S>.

5.text: Now Naaman<S>5283</S>, captain<S>8269</S> of the host<S>6635</S> of the king<S>4428</S> of Syria<S>758</S>, was a great<S>1419</S> man<S>376</S> with<S>6440</S> his master<S>113</S>, and honourable<S>5375</S>, because by him the LORD<S>3068</S> had given<S>5414</S> deliverance<S>8668</S> unto Syria<S>758</S>: he was also a mighty<S>1368</S> man<S>376</S> in valour<S>2428</S>, but he was a leper<S>6879</S>. <sup>with: Heb. before. honourable: or, gracious: Heb. lifted up, or, accepted in countenance. deliverance: or, victory</sup>

And the Syrians<S>758</S> had gone out<S>3318</S> by companies<S>1416</S>, and had brought away captive<S>7617</S> out of the land<S>776</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> a little<S>6996</S> maid<S>5291</S>; and she waited on<S>6440</S> Naaman's<S>5283</S> wife<S>802</S>. <sup>waited: Heb. was before</sup>

And she said<S>559</S> unto her mistress<S>1404</S>, Would<S>305</S> God my lord<S>113</S> were with<S>6440</S> the prophet<S>5030</S> that is in Samaria<S>8111</S>! for<S>227</S> he would recover<S>622</S> him of his leprosy<S>6883</S>. <sup>with: Heb. before</sup> <sup>recover: Heb. gather in</sup>
And one went in<S>935</S>, and told<S>5046</S> his lord<S>113</S>, saying<S>559</S>, Thus and thus said<S>1696</S> the maid<S>5291</S> that is of the land<S>776</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>.

And the king<S>4428</S> of Syria<S>758</S> said<S>559</S>, Go to<S>3212</S>, go<S>935</S>, and I will send<S>7971</S> a letter<S>5612</S> unto the king<S>4428</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>. And he departed<S>3212</S>, and took<S>3947</S> with him<S>3027</S> ten<S>6235</S> talents<S>3603</S> of silver<S>3701</S>, and six<S>8337</S> thousand<S>505</S> pieces of gold<S>2091</S>, and ten<S>6235</S> changes<S>2487</S> of raiment<S>899</S>. <sup>with: Heb. in his hand</sup>

And he brought<S>935</S> the letter<S>5612</S> to the king<S>4428</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, saying<S>559</S>, Now when this letter<S>5612</S> is come<S>935</S> unto thee, behold, I have therewith sent<S>7971</S> Naaman<S>5283</S> my servant<S>5650</S> to thee, that thou mayest recover<S>622</S> him of his leprosy<S>6883</S>.

And it came to pass, when the king<S>4428</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> had read<S>7121</S> the letter<S>5612</S>, that he rent<S>7167</S> his clothes<S>899</S>, and said<S>559</S>, Am I God<S>430</S>, to kill<S>4191</S> and to make alive<S>2421</S>, that this man<S>2088</S> doth send<S>7971</S> unto me to recover<S>622</S> a man<S>376</S> of his leprosy<S>6883</S>? wherefore<S>389</S> consider<S>3045</S>, I pray you, and see<S>7200</S> how he seeketh a quarrel<S>579</S> against me.

And it was so, when Elisha<S>477</S> the man<S>376</S> of God<S>430</S> had heard<S>8085</S> that the king<S>4428</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> had rent<S>7167</S> his clothes<S>899</S>, that he sent<S>7971</S> to the king<S>4428</S>, saying<S>559</S>, Wherefore hast thou rent<S>7167</S> thy clothes<S>899</S>? let him come<S>935</S> now to me, and he shall know<S>3045</S> that there is<S>3426</S> a prophet<S>5030</S> in Israel<S>3478</S>.
So Naaman<S>5283</S> came<S>935</S> with his horses<S>5483</S> and with his chariot<S>7393</S>, and stood<S>5975</S> at the door<S>6607</S> of the house<S>1004</S> of Elisha<S>477</S>.

And Elisha<S>477</S> sent<S>7971</S> a messenger<S>4397</S> unto him, saying<S>559</S>, Go<S>1980</S> and wash<S>7364</S> in Jordan<S>3383</S> seven<S>7651</S> times<S>6471</S>, and thy flesh<S>1320</S> shall come again<S>7725</S> to thee, and thou shalt be clean<S>2891</S>.

But Naaman<S>5283</S> was wroth<S>7107</S>, and went away<S>3212</S>, and said<S>559</S>, Behold, I thought<S>559</S>, He will surely<S>3318</S> come out<S>3318</S> to me, and stand<S>5975</S>, and call<S>7121</S> on the name<S>8034</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> his God<S>430</S>, and strike<S>5130</S> his hand<S>3027</S> over the place<S>4725</S>, and recover<S>622</S> the leper<S>6879</S>. <sup>I thought: Heb. I said. or, I said with my self, He will surely come out, etc.</sup> <sup>strike: Heb. move up and down</sup>

Are not Abana<S>71</S> <S>549</S> and Pharpar<S>6554</S>, rivers<S>5104</S> of Damascus<S>1834</S>, better<S>2896</S> than all the waters<S>4325</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>? may I not wash<S>7364</S> in them, and be clean<S>2891</S>? So he turned<S>6437</S> and went away<S>3212</S> in a rage<S>2534</S>. <sup>Abana: or, Amana</sup>

And his servants<S>5650</S> came near<S>5066</S>, and spake<S>1696</S> unto him, and said<S>559</S>, My father<S>1</S>, if the prophet<S>5030</S> had bid<S>1696</S> thee do some great<S>1419</S> thing<S>1697</S>, wouldest thou not have done<S>6213</S> it? how much rather then<S>637</S>, when he saith<S>559</S> to thee, Wash<S>7364</S>, and be clean<S>2891</S>?

Then went he down<S>3381</S>, and dipped<S>2881</S> himself seven<S>7651</S> times<S>6471</S> in Jordan<S>3383</S>, according to the saying<S>1697</S> of the man<S>376</S> of God<S>430</S>: and his flesh<S>1320</S> came again<S>7725</S> like unto the flesh<S>1320</S> of a little<S>6996</S> child<S>5288</S>, and he was clean<S>2891</S>.

And he returned<S>7725</S> to the man<S>376</S> of God<S>430</S>, he and all his company<S>4264</S>, and came<S>935</S>, and stood<S>5975</S> before<S>6440</S> him: and he said<S>559</S>, Behold, now I know<S>3045</S> that there is no God<S>430</S> in all the earth<S>776</S>, but in Israel<S>3478</S>: now therefore, I pray thee, take<S>3947</S> a blessing<S>1293</S> of thy servant<S>5650</S>.

But he said<S>559</S>, As the LORD<S>3068</S> liveth<S>2416</S>, before<S>6440</S> whom I stand<S>5975</S>, I will receive<S>3947</S> none. And he urged<S>6484</S> him to take<S>3947</S> it; but he refused<S>3985</S>.

And Naaman<S>5283</S> said<S>559</S>, Shall there not then, I pray thee, be given<S>5414</S> to thy servant<S>5650</S> two<S>6776</S> mules'<S>6505</S> burden<S>4853</S> of earth<S>127</S>? for thy servant<S>5650</S> will henceforth offer<S>6213</S> neither burnt offering<S>5930</S> nor sacrifice<S>2077</S> unto other<S>312</S> gods<S>430</S>, but unto the LORD<S>3068</S>.

In this thing<S>1697</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> pardon<S>5545</S> thy servant<S>5650</S>, that when my master<S>113</S> goeth<S>935</S> into the house<S>1004</S> of Rimmon<S>7417</S> to worship<S>7812</S> there, and he leaneth<S>8172</S> on my hand<S>3027</S>, and I bow<S>7812</S> myself in the house<S>1004</S> of Rimmon<S>7417</S>: when I bow down<S>7812</S> myself in the house<S>1004</S> of Rimmon<S>7417</S>, the LORD<S>3068</S> pardon<S>5545</S> thy servant<S>5650</S> in this thing<S>1697</S>.
And he said<S>559</S> unto him, Go<S>3212</S> in peace<S>7965</S>. So he departed<S>3212</S> from him a little<S>3530</S> way<S>776</S>. <sup>a little: Heb. a little piece of ground</sup>

But Gehazi<S>1522</S>, the servant<S>5288</S> of Elisha<S>477</S> the man<S>376</S> of God<S>430</S>, said<S>559</S>, Behold, my master<S>113</S> hath spared<S>2820</S> Naaman<S>5283</S> this Syrian<S>761</S>, in not receiving<S>3947</S> at his hands<S>3027</S> that which he brought<S>935</S>: but, as the LORD<S>3068</S> liveth<S>2416</S>, I will run<S>7323</S> after<S>310</S> him, and take<S>3947</S> somewhat<S>3972</S> of him.

So Gehazi<S>1522</S> followed<S>7291</S> after<S>310</S> Naaman<S>5283</S>. And when Naaman<S>5283</S> saw<S>7200</S> him running<S>7323</S> after<S>310</S> him, he lighted down<S>5307</S> from the chariot<S>4818</S> to meet<S>7125</S> him, and said<S>559</S>, Is all well<S>7965</S>? <sup>Is: Heb. Is there peace?</sup>

And he said<S>559</S>, All is well<S>7965</S>. My master<S>113</S> hath sent<S>7971</S> me, saying<S>559</S>, Behold, even now there be come<S>935</S> to me from mount<S>2022</S> Ephraim<S>669</S> two<S>8147</S> young men<S>5288</S> of the sons<S>1121</S> of the prophets<S>5030</S>: give<S>5414</S> them, I pray thee, a talent<S>3603</S> of silver<S>3701</S>, and two<S>8147</S> changes<S>2487</S> of garments<S>899</S>.

And Naaman<S>5283</S> said<S>559</S>, Be content<S>2974</S>, take<S>3947</S> two talents<S>3603</S>. And he urged<S>6555</S> him, and bound<S>6696</S> two<S>8147</S> talents<S>3603</S> of silver<S>3701</S> in two<S>8147</S> bags<S>2754</S>, with two<S>8147</S> changes<S>2487</S> of garments<S>899</S>, and laid<S>5414</S> them upon two<S>8147</S> of his servants<S>5288</S>; and they bare<S>5375</S> them before<S>6440</S> him.

And when he came<S>935</S> to the tower<S>6076</S>, he took<S>3947</S> them from their hand<S>3027</S>, and bestowed<S>6485</S> them in the house<S>1004</S>: and he let the men<S>582</S> go<S>7971</S>, and they departed<S>3212</S>. <sup>tower: or, secret place</sup>

But he went in<S>935</S>, and stood<S>5975</S> before his master<S>113</S>. And Elisha<S>477</S> said<S>559</S> unto him, Whence<S>370</S> comest thou, Gehazi<S>1522</S>? And he said<S>559</S>, Thy servant<S>5650</S> went<S>1980</S> no whither<S>575</S>. <sup>no whither: Heb. not hither or thither</sup>

And he said<S>559</S> unto him, Went<S>1980</S> not mine heart<S>3820</S> with thee, when the man<S>376</S> turned<S>2015</S> again from his chariot<S>4818</S> to meet<S>7125</S> thee? Is it a time<S>6256</S> to receive<S>3947</S> money<S>3701</S>, and to receive<S>3947</S> garments<S>899</S>, and oliveyards<S>2132</S>, and vineyards<S>3754</S>, and sheep<S>6629</S>, and oxen<S>1241</S>, and menservants<S>5650</S>, and maidservants<S>8198</S>?

The leprosy<S>6883</S> therefore of Naaman<S>5283</S> shall cleave<S>1692</S> unto thee, and unto thy seed<S>2233</S> for ever<S>5769</S>. And he went out<S>3318</S> from his presence<S>6440</S> a leper<S>6879</S> as white as snow<S>7950</S>.

6.text: And the sons<S>1121</S> of the prophets<S>5030</S> said<S>559</S> unto<S>6440</S> Elisha<S>477</S>, Behold now, the place<S>4725</S> where we dwell<S>3427</S> with thee is too strait<S>6862</S> for us.

Let us go<S>3212</S>, we pray thee, unto Jordan<S>3383</S>, and take<S>3947</S> thence every man<S>376</S> a<S>259</S> beam<S>6982</S>, and let us make<S>6213</S> us a place<S>4725</S> there, where we may dwell<S>3427</S>. And he answered<S>559</S>, Go<S>3212</S> ye.
And one<S>259</S> said<S>559</S>, Be content<S>2974</S>, I pray thee, and go<S>3212</S> with thy servants<S>5650</S>. And he answered<S>559</S>, I will go<S>3212</S>.
So he went<S>3212</S> with them. And when they came<S>935</S> to Jordan<S>3383</S>, they cut down<S>1504</S> wood<S>6086</S>.

But as one<S>259</S> was felling<S>5307</S> a beam<S>6982</S>, the axe head<S>1270</S> fell<S>5307</S> into the water<S>4325</S>: and he cried<S>6817</S>, and said<S>559</S>, Alas<S>162</S>, master<S>113</S>! for it was borrowed<S>7592</S>. <sup>axe head: Heb. iron</sup>

And the man<S>376</S> of God<S>430</S> said<S>559</S>, Where<S>575</S> fell<S>5307</S> it? And he shewed<S>7200</S> him the place<S>4725</S>. And he cut down<S>7094</S> a stick<S>6086</S>, and cast<S>7993</S> it in thither; and the iron<S>1270</S> did swim<S>6687</S>.
Therefore said<S>559</S> he, Take it up<S>7311</S> to thee. And he put out<S>7971</S> his hand<S>3027</S>, and took<S>3947</S> it.

Then the king<S>4428</S> of Syria<S>758</S> warred<S>3898</S> against Israel<S>3478</S>, and took counsel<S>3289</S> with his servants<S>5650</S>, saying<S>559</S>, In such<S>6423</S> and such<S>492</S> a place<S>4725</S> shall be my camp<S>8466</S>. <sup>camp: or, encamping</sup>

And the man<S>376</S> of God<S>430</S> sent<S>7971</S> unto the king<S>4428</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, saying<S>559</S>, Beware<S>8104</S> that thou pass<S>5674</S> not such<S>2088</S> a place<S>4725</S>; for thither the Syrians<S>758</S> are come down<S>5185</S>.

And the king<S>4428</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> sent<S>7971</S> to the place<S>4725</S> which the man<S>376</S> of God<S>430</S> told<S>559</S> him and warned<S>2094</S> him of, and saved<S>8104</S> himself there, not once<S>259</S> nor twice<S>8147</S>.

Therefore the heart<S>3820</S> of the king<S>4428</S> of Syria<S>758</S> was sore troubled<S>5590</S> for this thing<S>1697</S>; and he called<S>7121</S> his servants<S>5650</S>, and said<S>559</S> unto them, Will ye not shew<S>5046</S> me which of us is for the king<S>4428</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>?

And one<S>259</S> of his servants<S>5650</S> said<S>559</S>, None, my lord<S>113</S>, O king<S>4428</S>: but Elisha<S>477</S>, the prophet<S>5030</S> that is in Israel<S>3478</S>, telleth<S>5046</S> the king<S>4428</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> the words<S>1697</S> that thou speakest<S>1696</S> in thy bedchamber<S>2315</S> <S>4904</S>. <sup>None: Heb. No</sup>

And he said<S>559</S>, Go<S>3212</S> and spy<S>7200</S> where<S>351</S> he is, that I may send<S>7971</S> and fetch<S>3947</S> him. And it was told<S>5046</S> him, saying<S>559</S>, Behold, he is in Dothan<S>1886</S>.

Therefore sent<S>7971</S> he thither horses<S>5483</S>, and chariots<S>7393</S>, and a great<S>3515</S> host<S>2428</S>: and they came<S>935</S> by night<S>3915</S>, and compassed<S>5362</S> the city<S>5892</S> about<S>5362</S>. <sup>great: Heb. heavy</sup>

And when the servant<S>8334</S> of the man<S>376</S> of God<S>430</S> was risen<S>6965</S> early<S>7925</S>, and gone forth<S>3318</S>, behold, an host<S>2428</S> compassed<S>5437</S> the city<S>5892</S> both with horses<S>5483</S> and chariots<S>7393</S>. And his servant<S>5288</S> said<S>559</S> unto him, Alas<S>162</S>, my master<S>113</S>! how shall we do<S>6213</S>? <sup>the servant: or, the minister</sup>
And he answered<S>559</S>, Fear<S>3372</S> not: for they that be with us are more<S>7227</S> than they that be with them.

And Elisha<S>477</S> prayed<S>6419</S>, and said<S>559</S>, LORD<S>3068</S>, I pray thee, open<S>6491</S> his eyes<S>5869</S>, that he may see<S>7200</S>. And the LORD<S>3068</S> opened<S>6491</S> the eyes<S>5869</S> of the young man<S>5288</S>; and he saw<S>7200</S>: and, behold, the mountain<S>2022</S> was full<S>4390</S> of horses<S>5483</S> and chariots<S>7393</S> of fire<S>784</S> round about<S>5439</S> Elisha<S>477</S>.

And when they came down<S>3381</S> to him, Elisha<S>477</S> prayed<S>6419</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, and said<S>559</S>, Smite<S>5221</S> this people<S>1471</S>, I pray thee, with blindness<S>5575</S>. And he smote<S>5221</S> them with blindness<S>5575</S> according to the word<S>1697</S> of Elisha<S>477</S>.

And Elisha<S>477</S> said<S>559</S> unto them, This is not the way<S>1870</S>, neither is this<S>2090</S> the city<S>5892</S>: follow<S>3212</S> <S>310</S> me, and I will bring<S>3212</S> you to the man<S>376</S> whom ye seek<S>1245</S>. But he led<S>3212</S> them to Samaria<S>8111</S>. <sup>follow: Heb. come ye after me</sup>

And it came to pass, when they were come<S>935</S> into Samaria<S>8111</S>, that Elisha<S>477</S> said<S>559</S>, LORD<S>3068</S>, open<S>6491</S> the eyes<S>5869</S> of these men, that they may see<S>7200</S>. And the LORD<S>3068</S> opened<S>6491</S> their eyes<S>5869</S>, and they saw<S>7200</S>; and, behold, they were in the midst<S>8432</S> of Samaria<S>8111</S>.
And the king<S>4428</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> said<S>559</S> unto Elisha<S>477</S>, when he saw<S>7200</S> them, My father<S>1</S>, shall I smite<S>5221</S> them? shall I smite<S>5221</S> them?

And he answered<S>559</S>, Thou shalt not smite<S>5221</S> them: wouldest thou smite<S>5221</S> those whom thou hast taken captive<S>7617</S> with thy sword<S>2719</S> and with thy bow<S>7198</S>? set<S>7760</S> bread<S>3899</S> and water<S>4325</S> before<S>6440</S> them, that they may eat<S>398</S> and drink<S>8354</S>, and go<S>3212</S> to their master<S>113</S>.

And he prepared<S>3739</S> great<S>1419</S> provision<S>3740</S> for them: and when they had eaten<S>398</S> and drunk<S>8354</S>, he sent them away<S>7971</S>, and they went<S>3212</S> to their master<S>113</S>. So the bands<S>1416</S> of Syria<S>758</S> came<S>935</S> no more<S>3254</S> into the land<S>776</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>.

And it came to pass after this<S>310</S>, that Benhadad<S>1130</S> king<S>4428</S> of Syria<S>758</S> gathered<S>6908</S> all his host<S>4264</S>, and went up<S>5927</S>, and besieged<S>6696</S> Samaria<S>8111</S>.

And there was a great<S>1419</S> famine<S>7458</S> in Samaria<S>8111</S>: and, behold, they besieged<S>6696</S> it, until an ass's<S>2543</S> head<S>7218</S> was sold for fourscore<S>8084</S> pieces of silver<S>3701</S>, and the fourth part<S>7255</S> of a cab<S>6894</S> of dove's dung<S>1686</S> <S>3123</S> <S>2755</S> for five<S>2568</S> pieces of silver<S>3701</S>.

And as the king<S>4428</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> was passing by<S>5674</S> upon the wall<S>2346</S>, there cried<S>6817</S> a woman<S>802</S> unto him, saying<S>559</S>, Help<S>3467</S>, my lord<S>113</S>, O king<S>4428</S>.

And he said<S>559</S>, If the LORD<S>3068</S> do not help<S>3467</S> thee, whence<S>370</S> shall I help<S>3467</S> thee? out of the barnfloor<S>1637</S>, or out of the winepress<S>3342</S>? <sup>If: or, Let not the LORD save thee</sup>

And the king<S>4428</S> said<S>559</S> unto her, What aileth thee? And she answered<S>559</S>, This woman<S>802</S> said<S>559</S> unto me, Give<S>5414</S> thy son<S>1121</S>, that we may eat<S>398</S> him to day<S>3117</S>, and we will eat<S>398</S> my son<S>1121</S> to morrow<S>4279</S>.

So we boiled<S>1310</S> my son<S>1121</S>, and did eat<S>398</S> him: and I said<S>559</S> unto her on the next<S>312</S> day<S>3117</S>, Give<S>5414</S> thy son<S>1121</S>, that we may eat<S>398</S> him: and she hath hid<S>2244</S> her son<S>1121</S>. <sup>next: Heb. other</sup>

And it came to pass, when the king<S>4428</S> heard<S>8085</S> the words<S>1697</S> of the woman<S>802</S>, that he rent<S>7167</S> his clothes<S>899</S>; and he passed by<S>5674</S> upon the wall<S>2346</S>, and the people<S>5971</S> looked<S>7200</S>, and, behold, he had sackcloth<S>8242</S> within<S>1004</S> upon his flesh<S>1320</S>.

Then he said<S>559</S>, God<S>430</S> do<S>6213</S> so and more<S>3254</S> also to me, if the head<S>7218</S> of Elisha<S>477</S> the son<S>1121</S> of Shaphat<S>8202</S> shall stand<S>5975</S> on him this day<S>3117</S>.
